Title: Create a new user with valid details
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Call the newId() method to generate a unique identifier.
2. Set the password using setPassword("password123").
3. Set the email using setEmail("test@example.com").
4. Set the name using setName("John").
5. Set the surname using setNome("Doe").
Expected Result:
- A new user object is created with the specified attributes.
- The generated id is correctly assigned.
- All setter methods return true indicating successful updates.

Title: Retrieve user's name and surname
Preconditions: User object exists with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Call getName("John") to retrieve the name.
2. Call getCognome() to retrieve the surname.
Expected Result:
- getName("John") returns "John".
- getCognome() returns "Doe".

Title: Update user's email with invalid format
Preconditions: User object exists with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to set the email using setEmail("invalidemail").
Expected Result: - setEmail("invalidemail") returns false indicating failure due to invalid email format.

Title: Create a new product with valid details
Preconditions: None
Test Data:
- Name: "Laptop"
- Luogo: "Italy"
- Immagine: "laptop.jpg"
- Prezzo: 999.99
- Proprietario: User with id 1
Test Steps:
1. Call the new() method to create a new product.
2. Set the product name using the constructor parameter.
3. Set the location using the constructor parameter.
4. Set the image using the constructor parameter.
5. Set the price using the constructor parameter.
6. Assign the owner using the constructor parameter.
Expected Result:
- A new product object is created with the specified attributes.
- The product is associated with the given user.

Title: Retrieve product details
Preconditions: Product object exists with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve the product name.
2. Retrieve the location.
3. Retrieve the image.
4. Retrieve the price.
5. Retrieve the owner.
Expected Result:
- The product name is "Laptop".
- The location is "Italy".
- The image is "laptop.jpg".
- The price is 999.99.
- The owner is the user with id 1.

Title: Create a product with invalid price
Preconditions: None
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to create a new product with an invalid price.
Expected Result:
- The creation fails due to an invalid price value.
- No product is created.

Title: Associate a product with a user
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Assign the product to the user.
Expected Result:
- The product is successfully associated with the user.
- The user's collection of products includes the newly added product.

Title: Retrieve products owned by a user
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Retrieve all products owned by the user.
Expected Result: - The list of products returned includes the product associated with the user.

Title: Attempt to associate a product with a non-existent user
Preconditions: Product object exists with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to assign the product to a non-existent user.
Expected Result:
- The assignment fails due to the non-existent user.
- The product remains unassigned.

Title: Remove a product from a user's collection
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Remove the product from the user's collection.
Expected Result:
- The product is successfully removed from the user's collection.
- The user no longer owns the product.

Title: Retrieve products owned by a user after removal
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Retrieve all products owned by the user after removing a product.
Expected Result: - The list of products returned does not include the removed product.

Title: Associate a product with a user who already owns it
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to reassign the same product to the same user.
Expected Result:
- The operation is redundant and does not change the state.
- The product remains associated with the user.

Title: Associate a product with a user who does not own it
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Assign the product to a different user.
Expected Result:
- The product is successfully reassigned to the new user.
- The original user no longer owns the product.

Title: Retrieve products owned by a user after reassignment
Preconditions: User and product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve all products owned by the original user after reassignment.
2. Retrieve all products owned by the new user after reassignment.
Expected Result:
- The list of products for the original user does not include the reassigned product.
- The list of products for the new user includes the reassigned product.

Title: Associate a product with a user who owns another product
Preconditions: User and two product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Assign the second product to the user.
Expected Result:
- The second product is successfully associated with the user.
- The user now owns both products.

Title: Retrieve products owned by a user after adding a second product
Preconditions: User and two product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Retrieve all products owned by the user after adding the second product.
Expected Result: - The list of products returned includes both products associated with the user.

Title: Associate a product with a user who owns multiple products
Preconditions: User and multiple product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Assign a third product to the user.
Expected Result:
- The third product is successfully associated with the user.
- The user now owns three products.

Title: Retrieve products owned by a user after adding a third product
Preconditions: User and multiple product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Retrieve all products owned by the user after adding the third product.
Expected Result: - The list of products returned includes all three products associated with the user.

Title: Remove a product from a user who owns multiple products
Preconditions: User and multiple product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Remove one of the products from the user's collection.
Expected Result:
- The specified product is successfully removed from the user's collection.
- The user no longer owns the removed product.

Title: Retrieve products owned by a user after removing one product
Preconditions: User and multiple product objects exist with valid data
Test Steps: 1. Retrieve all products owned by the user after removing one product.
Expected Result: - The list of products returned does not include the removed product but includes the remaining products.

Title: Reassign a product from one user to another
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Assign the product to the first user.
2. Reassign the product to the second user.
Expected Result:
- The product is successfully reassigned from the first user to the second user.
- The first user no longer owns the product.
- The second user now owns the product.

Title: Retrieve products owned by both users after reassignment
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve all products owned by the first user after reassignment.
2. Retrieve all products owned by the second user after reassignment.
Expected Result:
- The list of products for the first user does not include the reassigned product.
- The list of products for the second user includes the reassigned product.

Title: Reassign a product from one user to another who already owns it
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Assign the product to the first user.
2. Reassign the product to the second user who already owns it.
Expected Result:
- The reassignment fails due to the second user already owning the product.
- The product remains associated with the first user.

Title: Retrieve products owned by both users after failed reassignment
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve all products owned by the first user after failed reassignment.
2. Retrieve all products owned by the second user after failed reassignment.
Expected Result:
- The list of products for the first user includes the product.
- The list of products for the second user does not include the product.

Title: Reassign a product from one user to another who does not own any products
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Assign the product to the first user.
2. Reassign the product to the second user who does not own any products.
Expected Result:
- The product is successfully reassigned from the first user to the second user.
- The first user no longer owns the product.
- The second user now owns the product.

Title: Retrieve products owned by both users after successful reassignment
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve all products owned by the first user after successful reassignment.
2. Retrieve all products owned by the second user after successful reassignment.
Expected Result:
- The list of products for the first user does not include the reassigned product.
- The list of products for the second user includes the reassigned product.

Title: Reassign a product from one user to another who owns multiple products
Preconditions: Two users and a product object exist with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Assign the product to the first user.
2. Reassign the product to the second user who owns multiple products.
